title: Aion NIF — Native Helper for In-VM Activities and Deterministic Helpers
intention: Make writing a native Rust function and exposing it to Gleam/Elixir workflows as a NIF obvious and safe: typed arguments in, a typed value out, no raw term arithmetic, no leaked heap pointers, no hand-rolled MFA registration. The crate serves the Tier-2 in-VM band of the execution model, and its entire organisation keeps the two uses of that band apart. (1) Deterministic helpers — JSON transform, template render, parse, format, crypto, math — called inline from workflow code, not recorded, re-executed verbatim on replay, safe because the same inputs always produce the same output. (2) Light in-VM activities — small side-effectful operations that run on beamr's dirty scheduler and MUST be invoked through the engine's activity contract so the result is recorded once and returned from history on replay, never re-executed. The crate makes the right thing easy and the wrong thing hard to express: a side-effectful NIF is typed as an activity from the first line and cannot be wired as an inline deterministic helper. When done, a host hands its NIF set to EngineBuilder::register_nifs and its Gleam workflows call deterministic ones inline and side-effectful ones as recorded activities, with the determinism guarantee preserved.
problem: beamr exposes a raw NIF surface (NativeFn = fn(&[Term], &mut ProcessContext) -> Result<Term, Term>, registered by module/function/arity into BifRegistryImpl, with a dirty-scheduler flag and request_suspend/wake_with_result async suspension), demonstrated ad hoc in beamr-meridian; writing one by hand is unpleasant and error-prone in ways that matter for a workflow engine.

Term wrangling is manual and repetitive — every NIF re-implements extract_string/extract_bytes/ok_binary/error_tuple/list-cons/map-decode, the conversions are subtle (binary vs atom, cons vs improper list), and a mistake is a runtime badarg, not a compile error.

Heap allocation for return values is a sharp edge: beamr-meridian documents that BIFs cannot touch the process heap for some return shapes and falls back to Box::leak — an unsafe-adjacent, per-call memory leak unacceptable in a long-running engine.

The deterministic/side-effectful distinction is invisible at the call site; nothing in the raw surface says whether a NIF is safe to call inline or must be a recorded activity, leaving the engine's single most important invariant (determinism) to author discipline. A native NIF that runs a shell command is a side effect; called inline it runs twice on replay and desynchronises history.

Registration is boilerplate — interning atoms, matching arities, threading the registry — repeated verbatim per NIF, with the engine expecting a specific shape from register_nifs. No shared, audited helper exists; Aion needs a reusable general-purpose crate with the determinism distinction baked into its types.

solution.summary: One Rust crate, aion-nif, layered so each layer is independently testable and the unsafe FFI surface is confined to a single audited module. The two-use split is the spine: a deterministic helper is declared with the deterministic builder, producing a Nif tagged Determinism::Pure for inline @external binding (not recorded); a side-effectful operation is declared with the activity builder, producing a Nif tagged Determinism::SideEffectful with the dirty-scheduler flag, registered such that the only way a workflow invokes it is through the engine's activity contract (activity.new in AF, dispatched by AE). Layer 1 (term): FromTerm/IntoTerm traits over beamr's term API with typed TermError, for ints, floats, bools, binaries, atoms, options, results, lists, maps. Layer 2 (payload): Payload<->Term via JSON content-type and a serde blanket so authors write NIFs over ordinary structs; this is where aion-nif depends on aion-core (Payload, ActivityError). Layer 3 (declare): deterministic_nif and activity_nif builders/macros that generate the NativeFn shim — arity check, per-argument typed decode, body call, heap-safe return encoding, panic containment — so the author never writes raw term code. Layer 4 (registry): a NifSet builder collecting descriptors into the value EngineBuilder::register_nifs consumes, validating no duplicate (module, function, arity). The raw module isolates any unsafe needed at the FFI heap-construction seam, documented per SAFETY block; the preferred state is zero unsafe via safe beamr APIs. aion-nif depends on beamr and aion-core only — never on the engine.

decisions.title: The two-use split is the spine, enforced by type
decisions.choice: The crate is organised entirely around the deterministic-helper vs recorded-activity distinction. A deterministic helper is declared with the deterministic builder (tag Determinism::Pure, bound inline, never recorded). A side-effectful operation is declared with the activity builder (tag Determinism::SideEffectful, dirty flag, native body returning Result<T, ActivityError>), and the only way a workflow invokes it is through the engine's activity contract so its result becomes an ActivityCompleted event returned from history on replay. The split is the reason the crate exists.

decisions.title: Determinism is a typed property of every NIF, not a convention
decisions.choice: Each declared NIF carries a Determinism tag in its descriptor. The deterministic builder can only produce Pure; the activity builder can only produce SideEffectful. AE's registration path reads the tag to choose binding mode: Pure NIFs are exposed for inline binding, SideEffectful only as activity bodies. A side-effectful NIF therefore cannot be wired as an inline deterministic helper. Rejected a single untyped nif! macro with a doc note 'don't call I/O ones inline': it leaves the engine's core invariant to author memory and review — the exact failure this crate exists to prevent.

decisions.title: Term conversion via FromTerm/IntoTerm traits
decisions.choice: A pair of traits over beamr's term API: FromTerm fallibly decodes a Term (with ProcessContext for atom resolution) into a Rust type with a typed TermError rather than a bare badarg; IntoTerm encodes a Rust value into a Term, allocating on the process heap through the context. Implemented for signed/unsigned ints, floats, bools, binaries (Vec<u8>/String), atoms (a small Atom-name newtype), Option (nil/value), Result-shaped {ok,T}/{error,E}, homogeneous lists (Vec<T>), and string-keyed maps. Built directly on beamr's Binary, Tuple, alloc_cons, alloc_tuple, term_to_value/value_to_term.

decisions.title: Payload/JSON bridge carries workflow values across the boundary
decisions.choice: Workflow values cross as aion-core::Payload (the type-erased serialised value). The payload module converts Payload<->Term via the JSON content-type (beamr's term_to_value to serde_json::Value to Payload and back), and provides FromTerm/IntoTerm blanket support for any T: Serialize + DeserializeOwned through Payload, so an author writes a NIF over ordinary Rust structs and gets term marshalling for free. This layer is where aion-nif depends on aion-core (Payload, and ActivityError for the side-effectful error).

decisions.title: The macro generates the shim; the author never writes raw term code
decisions.choice: deterministic_nif and activity_nif take a Rust fn/closure with FromTerm arguments and an IntoTerm (or Result<T, ActivityError>) return and generate the NativeFn shim: arity check, per-argument typed decode (with the failing argument index in the error), body call, heap-safe return encoding, and panic-to-error containment. This removes the entire class of hand-rolled term bugs and the Box::leak heap hazard from author code. Rejected a thin macro that still hands the author &[Term]: it would leave the sharp edges exposed and defeat the crate's purpose.

decisions.title: Side-effectful NIFs are dirty-scheduled and return ActivityError
decisions.choice: The activity builder tags the descriptor SideEffectful and sets the dirty-scheduler flag on by default (side effects may block, and a blocking call must not occupy a normal scheduler). The native body returns Result<T, ActivityError> (aion-core) so a failure carries the retryable/terminal classification AE/AT consult; it never returns a bare string error. Where suspension is required, the crate exposes beamr's existing request_suspend/wake_with_result mechanism (as beamr-meridian demonstrates) rather than inventing a new async model.

decisions.title: aion-nif emits a descriptor set; AE performs registration
decisions.choice: A NifSet builder collects declared Nifs under a module name into the value EngineBuilder::register_nifs consumes (per-NIF: module/function/arity, NativeFn, dirty flag, Determinism tag). aion-nif does NOT call beamr's BifRegistryImpl::register itself — AE's runtime module (the sole beamr boundary, AE D1) performs the actual registration, reading the tags to decide binding mode. This keeps the beamr-registration import confined to the engine and lets aion-nif stay a pure declaration library. NifSet rejects a duplicate (module, function, arity) at build time, never silently overwrites.

decisions.title: unsafe is isolated and justified at the FFI seam, not blanket-denied
decisions.choice: CLAUDE.md mandates unsafe_code = deny. The preferred outcome is zero unsafe: alloc_cons and alloc_tuple already exist on ProcessContext, so safe term construction is the default path. If and only if a return shape has no safe beamr path, the unsafe lives in the raw module behind safe wrappers, via a single module-level #![allow(unsafe_code)] documented as the audited FFI seam, with every unsafe block carrying a // SAFETY: comment stating the invariant it upholds. No unsafe outside raw. Rejected a crate-wide allow(unsafe_code) (it would let unaudited unsafe leak into conversion and declaration code); also rejected forcing every NIF through Box::leak as beamr-meridian does today (it leaks process memory per call and is not production-acceptable for a long-running engine). Eliminating the last unsafe by extending beamr's safe heap-alloc API is tracked as a beamr dependency, not done here.

decisions.title: aion-nif depends on beamr and aion-core only, never on the engine
decisions.choice: The crate depends on beamr (term/NIF API) and aion-core (Payload, ActivityError) and nothing else in the Aion tree. It does not import aion (the engine): it produces a descriptor set the engine consumes. This resolves COMPONENT-ARCHITECTURE's open question 'fold into aion or keep separate' as keep separate, so an in-VM activity author who only writes NIFs does not pull the whole engine.

constraints.description: unsafe_code = deny at the crate root. Any unsafe is confined to raw.rs behind a single documented, audited module exception; every unsafe block carries a // SAFETY: comment. No unsafe outside raw.rs. Preferred state is zero unsafe (safe beamr APIs only).
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses anywhere, with the sole explicit exception of the documented #![allow(unsafe_code)] FFI seam in raw.rs (CO1). No other bypass of any kind.
lib.rs is declarations and re-exports only.
aion-nif depends on beamr (term/NIF API) and aion-core (Payload, ActivityError) only. It does NOT depend on aion (the engine); it emits a descriptor set the engine consumes.

Determinism is a typed property: the deterministic builder yields only Determinism::Pure, the activity builder yields only Determinism::SideEffectful. No untyped declaration path. A side-effectful NIF cannot be expressed as an inline deterministic helper.
A side-effectful NIF's native body returns Result<T, ActivityError> (aion-core) so a failure carries the retryable/terminal classification AE/AT consult. It never returns a bare string error.

Generated shims never expose raw &[Term] to author code, never Box::leak in author-reachable paths, and contain panics raised by the author body, converting them to a typed NIF error rather than unwinding across the FFI boundary.
NifSet rejects a duplicate (module, function, arity) at build time with a typed NifDeclError, never silently overwrites.

aion-nif registers nothing with beamr itself. It emits a descriptor set; AE's runtime module (the sole beamr boundary) performs the actual registration. aion-nif must not call BifRegistryImpl::register.

goals: FromTerm/IntoTerm traits over beamr's term API with a typed TermError, covering ints, floats, bools, binaries, atoms, options, results, homogeneous lists, and string-keyed maps.
Payload<->Term bridge via the JSON content-type, plus a serde blanket so NIFs can be written over ordinary Serialize/DeserializeOwned Rust types.

deterministic_nif builder/macro producing a Determinism::Pure Nif whose generated shim does arity check, typed per-argument decode, body call, and heap-safe return encoding — the inline, never-recorded path.
activity_nif builder/macro producing a Determinism::SideEffectful Nif with the dirty-scheduler flag and a Result<T, ActivityError> body — the recorded, activity-contract-only path.
Generated shims contain author panics and convert them to a typed NIF error rather than unwinding across the FFI boundary.
NifSet builder collecting descriptors into the value EngineBuilder::register_nifs consumes, validating no duplicate (module, function, arity).
All unsafe (if any) confined to the raw module behind safe, // SAFETY:-documented wrappers; the rest of the crate is safe Rust; preferred state is zero unsafe.
A minimal illustrative NIF set (one deterministic, one side-effectful) that proves the full contract end-to-end against the in-VM execution path.
non_goals: No engine, no activity recording, no retry logic — AE owns activity dispatch and the beamr boundary, AD owns event recording, AT owns retry decisions and durable waits. aion-nif provides the native bodies and descriptor set those consume.
No Gleam SDK / @external bindings — AF (aion_flow) defines the Gleam functions that bind to these NIFs at runtime. This is the Rust side of that contract.
No remote / out-of-process workers — Tier-3 workers in Python/TS/Rust are AR, a different mechanism over the wire protocol. NIFs are in-VM native code only.
No changes to beamr's NIF runtime — this is a helper over the beamr surface; extending beamr (e.g. a safe heap-alloc API to remove the last unsafe) is beamr's work, referenced as a dependency.
No domain-specific NIFs — this crate ships the machinery and a minimal illustrative set only; Meridian's concrete NIFs (run_step_norn, commit, ...) live in the Meridian tree using this crate.
No new async/await NIF model — side-effectful NIFs use beamr's dirty-scheduler flag and the existing request_suspend/wake_with_result mechanism; the crate exposes it, it does not invent one.

structure.crates/aion-nif/src/raw.rs: [AN-001, AN-006] isolated, documented FFI seam (heap term build)
structure.crates/aion-nif/src/term.rs: [AN-002] FromTerm/IntoTerm traits + primitive impls (ints, floats, bools, binaries, atoms)
structure.crates/aion-nif/src/term_collection.rs: [AN-002] list + map + option + result term impls
structure.crates/aion-nif/src/payload.rs: [AN-003] Payload<->Term bridge; serde blanket via Payload
structure.crates/aion-nif/src/descriptor.rs: [AN-004] Nif descriptor + Determinism tag + dirty flag
structure.crates/aion-nif/src/registry.rs: [AN-006] NifSet builder; the value register_nifs consumes
